compressedText:
# 72-Hour Power-Outage Field Guide

Goal: household passes up to 72 hours of outage safely and comfortably. Temperate climate, no backup generator assumed. Do sections in order; earlier ones matter most.

## Water
- Store 4 L per person daily: 12 L per person over 72 hours.
- Half for drinking; half for cooking and hygiene.
- Food-grade containers, out of direct sunlight; replace every 6 months.
- Given advance storm warning, also fill the bathtub: for flushing toilets and washing, never drinking.

## Food
- Dedicated shelf, 3 days' non-perishables: canned beans, canned fish, peanut butter, crackers, dried fruit, shelf-stable milk.
- Choose no-cook foods; own a manual can opener (electric useless).
- Eat fridge perishables first, then freezer, then emergency shelf.

## Refrigerator and freezer
- Unopened fridge keeps food safe ~4 hours.
- Full freezer stays safe ~48 hours, half-full only ~24; keep it consolidated and full, even with containers of frozen water.
- Keep both doors closed; tape a reminder note to each.
- Discard perishables over 2 hours above 4°C; when in doubt, throw it out.

## Light
- Primary light: battery-powered LED lanterns or headlamps. Avoid candles: leading cause of house fires during outages.
- At least one light per person, two spare battery sets per light.
- Headlamp beside each bed, lantern in kitchen; place all findable in the dark.

## Heat and safety
- Never run camping stove, barbecue, or generator indoors, in garage, or near window: each emits odourless, potentially fatal carbon monoxide.
- Install at least one battery-powered carbon monoxide alarm on each floor where people sleep.
- In cold, gather everyone in one small south-facing room, door closed; layered clothing and blankets beat any improvised heater.
- Below 10°C indoors and unable to stay warm, relocate to a warming centre or neighbour's home.

## Communication
- Keep battery-powered or hand-crank radio; learn local emergency station's frequency before outage.
- Charge every phone and power bank whenever storm forecast; a full 20,000 mAh bank recharges a typical phone ~4 times.
- Phones to low-power mode at outage start.
- Pre-agree one out-of-area contact everyone texts; texts beat voice calls on congested networks.

## Medical
- First-aid kit and 7-day supply of every prescription; rotate to stay in date.
- Refrigerated medication like insulin generally lasts up to 28 days at room temperature; confirm your drug with a pharmacist beforehand.
- Pharmacy, doctor, and poison-control numbers on paper; a dead phone is not a phone book.

## When power returns
- Wait 5 minutes before switching major appliances on, letting grid stabilise.
- Check freezer: food still holding ice crystals can be safely refrozen.
- Restock everything used within one week, starting with water and batteries.
- One-page note on what was missing or awkward; fix at least one gap before next outage.
hardConstraintNote: The candidate remains a self-contained instruction document: full title naming the 72-hour power-outage scope, an intro stating goal, assumptions (temperate climate, no generator) and reading order, and all eight sections with imperative, actionable bullets. Every instruction is executable without consulting any other document — no fact, threshold, or step was externalised, only wording tightened. A reader holding only this text can prepare, endure, and recover from the outage exactly as with the 526-word source.
rationale: Applies the validated line-editor factor (573→534, structural, runs/r3/r3.1/p1- per frontier.json OT-2 evidence) over the folded frontier-best text (artifact/GUIDE.compressed.md, 526 by frontier.best.metric). All cuts are wording-level, never fact-level: (1) free-standing punctuation tokens removed — the bare '=' in the water line and three bare '—' tokens become attached colons/semicolons (4 words); (2) restatement removed — 'fill bathtub — bathtub water for...' loses the duplicated 'bathtub water' (the referent is unambiguous); (3) filler adverbs cut — 'entirely', 'as much as possible', 'the full', 'basic' (emphasis, not content); (4) connective tissue tightened — 'Agree in advance on'→'Pre-agree', 'If indoor temperature falls below 10°C and you cannot stay warm'→'Below 10°C indoors and unable to stay warm', 'pick one small room on south-facing side, close door, gather everyone'→'gather everyone in one small south-facing room, door closed'; (5) title and two headers shortened (headers carry navigation, not testable facts; 'Refrigerator and freezer' and 'Heat and safety' kept in full). Every quantity, duration, temperature, named item, list member, and ordered step in the source survives: 4 L/person/day and 12 L/person/72 h, drinking-vs-cooking split, food-grade containers out of sunlight, 6-month rotation, advance-warning bathtub fill with never-drink restriction, the six shelf foods, manual can opener with electric-useless note, fridge→freezer→shelf eat order, ~4 h fridge, ~48 h full / ~24 h half freezer with frozen-water consolidation, doors closed + taped reminder notes, 2 h/4°C discard rule with when-in-doubt maxim, LED lanterns/headlamps primary, candles as leading cause of outage house fires, one light per person + two spare battery sets per light, headlamp per bed + kitchen lantern findable in dark, CO triple prohibition (stove/barbecue/generator × indoors/garage/window) with odourless-fatal warning, at-least-one CO alarm per sleeping floor, small south-facing room + layers-beat-heaters, 10°C relocation threshold to warming centre or neighbour, battery/hand-crank radio + pre-learned emergency frequency, charge-on-forecast + 20,000 mAh ≈ 4 phone charges, low-power mode at start, one out-of-area text contact + texts-beat-calls, first-aid kit + 7-day prescriptions rotated in date, insulin 28 days at room temperature + pharmacist confirmation, paper numbers for pharmacy/doctor/poison control + dead-phone line, 5-minute appliance wait for grid stabilisation, ice-crystal refreeze test, one-week restock starting water and batteries, one-page after-action note + fix-one-gap. Counted 472 by the exact frontier rule via pipe (no files touched). KILLED_LEVERS.md contains no entries, so no K-id fence applies.
title: Composed line-edit over the folded 526-word best (OT-3): sentence-level compression, zero fact drops
